questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Directions: Choose the right one-word substitution from the given options.</span></p>

<p>Fear of books</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate proverb that conveys the meaning given in brackets in the following sentence, to fill in the blank.</p>

<p>Emma should stop being jealous of her friend&rsquo;s success and focus on her own capabilities. After all, _________ (You should concentrate on utilizing your own skills or strengths rather than desiring others&#39; advantages).</p>
options: the grass is always greener on the other side /
Rome wasn&#39;t built in a day /
every man is the architect of his own fortune /
all that glitters is not gold /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Direction: Choose the correct meaning of the given idiom:</span></p>

<p>Fool&#39;s errand</p>
options: to spend time idly, aimlessly, or frivolously /
an effort that is unlikely to be successful /
a state of happiness based on a person&#39;s not knowing about or denying the existence of potential trouble /
believes to be valuable, but that really is not /

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence.</p>

<p>UNESCO is <u>committed</u> to protecting individual privacy and securing the personal information made available to us when you visit unesco.org, as well as UNESCO pages on other sites.&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; background-color: (255, 255, 255);">Directions: Find the synonym of the given word?</span></p>

<p>PLACATE</p>

<p><strong>Direction:</strong><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">&nbsp;</span><span style="font-weight: 700;">Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response accordingly.</span></p>

<p>I <strong><u>rejoice</u></strong> to know that you are well and happy.&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom that can be substituted in the following sentence.</strong></p>

<p>I begged him to reconsider my application, but he&nbsp;<u>put his foot down.</u></p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Edge out</p>
<strong>In the following question four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelled. Find the incorrectly spelled word.</strong>
